VerbalDescription: a black grid one cell wide.  the height is equal to the number of single cells outside of the main object
a black grid one cell wide.  the height is equivalent to the number of coloured squares outside of the main object
a black grid one cell wide. the height is equal to the number of coloured cells around the edge of the main object
Count how many blocks are outside the shapes border. Make a grid that is 1 wide and as many high as blocks outside the border. Keep the grid black.

You will see several things in the input grid - a box, and then several single-square objects. Some of these single-square objects will be inside the box, some will be outside the box. Count ONLY the number of single-square objects that are OUTSIDE the box. Now, change your grid size to be a width of '1', and the height to be the number of single-square objects OUTSIDE the box. Do NOT color in any squares in your grid, simply submit it as-is. For example, if there are 3 single-square objects outside the box, your grid will be a blank grid with a width of 1 and a height of 3.

In the input grid, you will see several single-square objects, and a 'box'. Some of the single-square objects will be inside the box, some will be outside the box. Count the number that are OUTSIDE the box. Now, change your grid to be a single column (1 square wide) by the number of single-square objects outside the box. DO NOT COLOR ANY SQUARES IN THE GRID, submit it as-is. For example, if you see a box, with one single-square object inside, and three outside, your final result will be a blank grid that is a single square wide by three squares tall.

The vertical dimension should be the same number as the amount of smaller squares located OUTSIDE the bigger squares. Ignore everything else. Keep the color black. The horizontal dimensions should be 1.
The vertical dimensions should be the same number as the amount of smaller squares located OUTSIDE the bigger squares. Keep the color black. The horizontal dimension should be 1.
width is the same number as the amount of small squares outside the bigger square. height is equal to 1. color is black only.
The height of the new grid should be the same number as there are colored cells on the outside of the original and it should be all black.
Count the number of colored cells outside of the main shape in the task. This should be the height of the answer grid. The answer grid should have a width of 1 and be all black.
count the colors outside the box and use the number to resize the shape, that's it. so if there are three colored boxes outside the inside colored box. resize your shape, height 3, and width 1.
a black grid one cell wide.  the height is equal to the number of coloured cells outside of the main objects on the grid

Count the amount of colored squares that are outside the larger colored shape. Create a grid that is as many high as that amount of squares and only one square wide.
make the hight of the shape the amount of squares that are outside the main shape

In the input grid, you will see a box, and several single-square objects. Count ONLY the single-square objects that are OUTSIDE the box. Now, set your grid width to '1'. Set the height to the number of single-square objects OUTSIDE the box in the input grid. DO NOT COLOR IN ANY SQUARES. Submit the grid as-is, blank.
Set the vertical dimensions to the AMOUNT of single squares located outside the bigger square. Keep the horizontal dimension as 1. Everything else is irrelevant.
The vertical dimension should be the same number as the amount of smaller squares located OUTSIDE the bigger squares. Keep the color black. The horizontal dimension should be 1.
The height is the same number as the amount of smaller squares outside the bigger square. The width is 1. The color for all squares is black.
Count the colored cells on the outside of the shape. The new grid should have the height of that number and be all black.
Count the number of colored cells outside of the main shape in the task. This should be the height of the answer grid. The answer grid should have a width of 1 and be all black.

resize the grid size to the number of one cell object outside the main object

There will be items of two different colours. One will create rectangles and one colour will create single colour dots. These may be inside or outside of the rectangles. Count how many single dots are outside the rectangle(s). Resize the grid to that height and only 1 square wide and colour it black.

There will be single dots of colour and a rectangular/square shape of colour. Count how many dots of colour are outside of the rectangle/square. Resize the grid to be that many squares high and 1 square wide and colour them all black.
Create a new grid that is only black in colour. This should be one square wide and the height should be equal to the number of coloured dots outside of the sqaure/rectangle in the original image.

Count the amount of squares that are on the outside of the "hoop" shape or shapes. For the answer create a grid by stacking these squares on top of each other (the grid will be one square wide). Change the colour of the whole grid to the same colour as the background (black).
a black grid  one cell wide.  the height is equal to the number of single cells outside of the main object

make the grid the size of how many coloured squares are outside the main shape

In the input grid, you will see several single-square objects, and a 'box'. Some of the single-square objects will be inside the box, some will be outside the box. Count the number that are OUTSIDE the box. Now, change your grid to be a single column (1 square wide) by the number of single-square objects outside the box. DO NOT COLOR ANY SQUARES IN THE GRID, submit it as-is. Keep all of the colors within the bounds of the larger object.  Fill in the large object completely with its own color.  Areas of the secondary color falling outside the main shape need to be black.

The input should have one larger shape in outline form. The output is a copy of the input with this outlined shape fully visible and any colored block that was on or outside of that shape removed. The colored blocks inside the shape remain in the output.

Anything that is a different color needs to be inside the frame of whatever shape is displayed. If a line runs over the border of the frame, change any blocks going over the frame to the same color as the frame and anything blocks extending past the frame to black.
Use a grid of the same size and find the outline of same colored tiles that are connected, copy over that outline and any colored squares inside it while omitting any tiles that are outside of it.

Make the grid size 12 by 12. Place the large shape on the grid in the same color and position. Make the entire border of the shape the same color. Then remove any squares in the second color that are outside the shape, but keep all of them that are inside the shape.

You will see a shape of one color with lines or another color drawn over it. The shape remains the same, but the only keep the portion of the colored lines which are within the shape's border. All boxes on the main shape's border should be the same color as the shape. So draw the border first, then the lines inside the border.

Make the grid the same size as the original one, make the main border in the same color as the orginal and then make the lines in the middle with the same color it is in the orginal. Do not add the outside lines.

resize the grid to match the original. Look for the large pattern. Copy that with the same main color all around as the original. Now look at the interior and color the blocks the as the interior of the original, leave the exterior of the main pattern all one color, don't change the color of the big outside pattern. Anything outside of the big pattern don't copy.

Your image will be comprised of two colours. There will be a large central shape of one colour. This object will have blocks of another colour inside it, and outside of it. Simply copy the image test image, but remove any blocks from outside the main image. Your final image should a main shape e.g. a square, rhombus or whatever, with some coloured blocks inside. All coloured blocks outside the main shape should not be copied.
Keep the main shape the same size and color. Remove any of the add ons that are overlapping the main shape or on the outside of the main shape.

Remove all of the cubes that make up the linear structures outside of the shapes, and restore the shapes boundary lines.  The shape should be whole and of one colour (its original). Any piece of the lines that pierced into the shape are kept, so that small pieces of the lines only exist within the confines of the shape.
Keep the grid the same size. Remove all squares from outside of the shape. The ones inside can stay. If part of the shape is a different colour, change this back to ones that make the shape

The rule is that everthing outside the boundary o f the shape is removed, and that the border of the main shape is all the same colour. You should change the grid size to 12 high and 13 wide using the input box, and press the resize button. Yoy should then click on the copy input button. Make sure that black is selected, then press the edit buitton and click on any coloured grid squares outside the boundary of the shape. Then click on the blue colour, and click on edit, then click on the 4 grid squares that are red to change them to blue.

There will be two red squares and one yellow. If the red squares are above and below the yellow one, delete the red squares such that only the yellow square remains. If the red squares are left or right of the yellow square, delete the yellow square so that only the red ones remain. Keep the grid the same.

Remove the pixel with the lowest frequency colour if not on the same line as the pixels with the higher frequency colour.

If there is a yellow pixel below or even with the lowest red pixel, delete the yellow pixel. Otherwise, delete the red pixels.

Set the grid size to the same as the input grid. The input includes two red squares and a yellow square. If the red squares are in the same horizontal row, copy ONLY the red squares to the answer grid (same color and position). If the squares are in any other arrangement, copy ONLY the yellow square (same color and position).
Resize to same size. Remove shapes closest to corners.
Copy the grid. Remove the singular yellow block if it is outside of the two red blocks. If the yellow block is anywhere between the two red blocks on the same row, remove the red blocks.

Choose 'Copy from input' button. Choose 'Edit' and click the black color. If the two red shapes are on the same horizontal line (if they are left and right of each other), then click the yellow shape to erase it. If the two red shapes are not on the same horizontal line, then click the two red shapes to erase them.

You will see two red squares and one yellow. If the yellow square is outside the two red squares, remove it. If the yellow square is between the two red squares, remove the two red squares and keep only the yellow one.

Look at the input grid, there will be three singular colour shapes in two different colours. The answer is the same grid size as the input but the answer will depend on how the three colour blocks are placed. Take note of the two colour blocks that are the same colour. If you can draw a direct line between the two blocks of the same colour without the third block being in the way, the answer will be the same two blocks in the same colour and two locations on the grid. If when you draw a line between the two blocks of the same colour, the third block is in the way, you will draw just the singular colour block in the same location and colour as the input.

Edit the dimensions of the grid to be the same as shown in the test. If the yellow square is between the two red (in any direction) squares then only fill in where the yellow square is located. If the dot of a different color appears inside of the shape, only the dot of color appears in the solution area. Conversely, if the dot is outside of the shape, copy the shape into the solution area. Retain the same size grid and use the same color for the dot or shape in your solution.

Resize the grid to the same size as the test grid. Connect the red squares into a shape. If the yellow square is inside of the shape copy the yellow square from the test grid. If the yellow square is outside of the shape, copy the red squares from the test grid.

Set the dimensions to the same as the input. The input should consist of a perimeter of red dots, and a single yellow dot that is either inside or outside of the perimeter. If the yellow dot from the input is within the red perimeter, in the output only copy the yellow dot, putting it in the same place as the input. If the yellow dot from the input is outside the red perimeter, in the output only copy the red perimeter, putting it in the same place as the input.

Two objects are depicted, one in blue and one in green. Several small squares are depicted randomly scattered around the image in a third color. Count the number of these squares that land inside the boundaries of the blue and green objects, ignoring any that are outside of those boundaries or happen to be on top of those boundaries, and then create a grid with a height of one and a width corresponding to that total number. Finally, color the squares of the grid according to how many are found inside each box, using their respective colors. This can be interpreted as a score, or gauge, measuring how many colored squares each box contained.

In the input grid, you will see a shape that could be considered a 'cup' - it will have 2-3 sides and be open along the top edge. There will also be several single-square objects. First, copy the size and shape of the input grid. Now, you will copy over ONLY the single-square objects that are fully 'inside' the 'cup' object - you will not copy the 'cup', nor will you copy over any single-square objects that are outside the boundary of the 'cup', whether that is above it, or underneath it, or beside it, only the ones that are held within it.

You will see several objects in the input grid. One will be a large 2-3 sided outline.. imagine this is a 'cup'. Duplicate the size and shape of the input grid.
